preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true

column.name: title
column.value: Pathology Laboratory

column.name: objective
column.value: Complete pathology workflow from sample reception to archiving

column.name: protocol
column.value: Standard Pathology Laboratory Protocol

column.name: content
column.value: Pathology Laboratory notebook template with 8 predefined workflow pages for sample creation, QC, processing, testing, storage, reporting, disposal, and reference management.

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 1

changeSet.comment: Create Sample Creation and Metadata Capture page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Sample Creation & Metadata Capture

column.name: instructions
column.value: Create the pathology sample and capture all metadata at once. Enter: Sample Identity (unique accession number, barcode, category - Clinical diagnostic/Research), Sample Source (Alert Hospital/Research project, received date/time, received by), Specimen Type (Histopathology: FFPE block, fresh biopsy, surgical resection; Cytopathology: FNA, body fluids, urine, sputum, cervical smear; Hematology: EDTA blood; Research: human tissue, animal tissue, bacterial pellets, cell cultures), Clinical/Research Metadata (Patient ID, requesting clinician, collection date/time, specimen site, clinical details for clinical samples; Study ID, PI name, participant/animal ID, ethical approval reference for research samples). System generates unique ID and barcode, sets status to 'Created - Pending QC'.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 1

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 2

changeSet.comment: Create Sample Quality Control page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Sample Quality Control

column.name: instructions
column.value: Verify sample suitability before processing. Perform QC checks by sample type: Histology (fixative used - 10% NBF, fixative ratio 10:1, fixation duration, tissue integrity), Cytology (container integrity, preservative type e.g. Cytolyt, volume, clot presence), Peripheral blood (clot check in EDTA tube), Research samples (consent verified, storage medium OCT/RNAlater, sample type matches protocol). For tissue blocks post-embedding: check for smooth surface, correct depth/orientation, no paraffin overflow. Record QC outcome (Pass/Fail), remarks, staff initials, QC date. If Fail: select action (Recollection requested/Process with limitations), notify clinician/PI and lab head/pathologist.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 2

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 3

changeSet.comment: Create Sample Processing and Aliquoting page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Sample Processing & Aliquoting

column.name: instructions
column.value: Create slides, blocks, aliquots while maintaining traceability. Grossing/Aliquoting: Section tissue blocks into slides, aliquot fluids for LBC/cell blocks/molecular testing/biobanking. Label with parent accession number + unique child ID, maintain parent-child relationship. Processing by type: Histopathology (gross exam, description, sectioning, tissue processing through alcohols/xylene/paraffin, embedding, microtomy 3-5 um), Cytopathology (centrifugation, smears Pap/Romanowsky, cell block preparation from clots/pellets), Peripheral blood (wedge smear within hours, Giemsa/Wright stain), Research (per study SOPs - LN2 freezing, OCT embedding, nucleic acid extraction).

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 3

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 4

changeSet.comment: Create Testing, Staining and Microscopy page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Testing, Staining & Microscopy

column.name: instructions
column.value: Perform diagnostic or research assays on sample-derived material. Routine stains: H&E (histology), Papanicolaou/Romanowsky (cytology/blood). Special stains: AFB, GMS, PAS, Gram. Advanced techniques: IHC/ICC (FFPE blocks, cell blocks, smears), ISH. Research assays: Laser microdissection, multiplex IHC, immunofluorescent staining, RNAscope. Run positive/negative controls for each IHC/ICC/ISH run. Record assay acceptance based on control and stain quality. Clinical results must be verified by certified pathologist. Document: accession number, block/slide ID, test name, control results, result, signatures.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 4

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 5

changeSet.comment: Create Storage and Inventory Management page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Storage & Inventory Management

column.name: instructions
column.value: Track physical storage and retrieval of pathology materials. Storage conditions: FFPE blocks & slides at room temperature in labeled cabinets, Cell blocks same as FFPE, Residual cytology material at 4C for 2-4 weeks, Frozen research samples at -80C or LN2 vapor, Blood smears at room temperature in slide boxes. Location tracking: storage unit (cabinet/freezer), rack, box, position. Record date stored/retrieved and recipient signature. Environmental monitoring: Manual temperature checks twice daily, logged per storage unit.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 5

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 6

changeSet.comment: Create Reporting and Performance Monitoring page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Reporting & Performance Monitoring

column.name: instructions
column.value: Track laboratory performance and turnaround. Generate routine reports: Monthly specimen volume, Turnaround time (TAT), Rejection rates, QC incidents, Equipment downtime. Key metrics: Specimen rejection rate, Assay success rate, TAT, Equipment downtime. Data used for monthly QC meetings. System generates dashboards for performance visualization.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 6

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 7

changeSet.comment: Create Disposal and Archiving page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Disposal & Archiving

column.name: instructions
column.value: Close the sample lifecycle in compliance with policy. Disposal: Per retention policy (Clinical - national guidelines, Research - approved protocol). Record in Disposal Logbook: date, sample IDs, method (incineration/autoclaving), staff signature, unit head approval. Mark Storage Logbook as 'Disposed [Date]'. Archiving: Physical archives (logbooks, ledgers, reports) in fire-proof cabinets. Digital data backed up on secure servers. System marks sample as Closed and locks records.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 7

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.page_order = 8

changeSet.comment: Create Reference and SOP Module page for Pathology Laboratory notebook

column.name: title
column.value: Reference & SOP Module

column.name: instructions
column.value: Provide controlled access to SOPs and protocols. Features: SOP repository with dedicated access to reference materials. Protocol management: Upload new/updated protocols, maintain access to previous versions. Access control: View access for all users, Upload/edit access for authorized personnel only (lab managers, supervisors). SOPs uploaded and stored as reference documents. All users can view SOPs without restriction.

column.name: content
notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

delete.where: notebook_id = (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true) AND page_order = 8

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.workflow_page_template WHERE workflow_category = 'PATHOLOGY'

changeSet.comment: Insert predefined Pathology workflow page templates

column.name: name
column.value: Sample Creation & Metadata Capture

column.name: description
column.value: Create the pathology sample and capture all metadata at once

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Create samples with full metadata including sample identity, source, specimen type, and clinical/research metadata. System generates unique ID and barcode.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Creation and Metadata Capture - Bulk data entry for incoming pathology samples

column.name: name
column.value: Sample Quality Control

column.name: description
column.value: Verify sample suitability before processing

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Perform QC checks by sample type. Record QC outcome, remarks, and actions for failed samples.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Quality Control - Batch verification of sample quality

column.name: name
column.value: Sample Processing & Aliquoting

column.name: description
column.value: Create slides, blocks, aliquots while maintaining traceability

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Process samples by type (histopathology, cytopathology, blood, research). Create child samples and maintain parent-child relationships.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Processing and Aliquoting - Create derived samples with traceability

column.name: name
column.value: Testing, Staining & Microscopy

column.name: description
column.value: Perform diagnostic or research assays on sample-derived material

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Perform routine stains, special stains, and advanced techniques. Run controls and document results with pathologist verification.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Testing, Staining and Microscopy - Assay execution and result documentation

column.name: name
column.value: Storage & Inventory Management

column.name: description
column.value: Track physical storage and retrieval of pathology materials

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Assign storage locations, track retrieval, and log environmental monitoring data.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Storage and Inventory Management - Location tracking and environmental monitoring

column.name: name
column.value: Reporting & Performance Monitoring

column.name: description
column.value: Track laboratory performance and turnaround

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Generate routine reports, track key metrics, and document QC meetings.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Reporting and Performance Monitoring - Dashboards and metrics

column.name: name
column.value: Disposal & Archiving

column.name: description
column.value: Close the sample lifecycle in compliance with policy

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Record disposal with appropriate method and approvals. Archive physical and digital records.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Disposal and Archiving - Sample lifecycle closure

column.name: name
column.value: Reference & SOP Module

column.name: description
column.value: Provide controlled access to SOPs and protocols

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Access SOP repository, upload protocols, and manage version control.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Reference and SOP Module - Protocol management
